texto: Tech isn’t morally good or bad until it’s wielded by the corporations that fashion it for mass consumption. Apps and platforms can be designed to promote rich social connections; or, like cigarettes, they can be designed to addict. Today, unfortunately, many tech developments do promote addiction.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Adam Alter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a tempo. O trecho “Tech isn’t morally good or bad until it’s wielded by the corporations that fashion it for mass co…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, apps, social-media pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de social-media e technology-addiction.

frase_original: Tech isn’t morally good or bad until it’s wielded by the corporations that fashion it for mass consumption. Apps and platforms can be designed to promote rich social connections; or, like cigarettes, they can be designed to addict. Today, unfortunately, many tech developments do promote addiction.

texto: Walter Isaacson, who ate dinner with the Jobs family while researching his biography of Steve Jobs, told Bilton that, “No one ever pulled out an iPad or computer. The kids did not seem addicted at all to devices.” It seemed as if the people producing tech products were following the cardinal rule of drug dealing: never get high on your own supply.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Adam Alter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Walter Isaacson, who ate dinner with the Jobs family while researching his biography of Steve Job…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, addictive-behaviour, technology pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de technology.
